mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 45% of low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending corporate earnings declines, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE remains below 3.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in more than 50% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance with p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, generating positive short-term S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during tightening cycles, producing negative 5-day returns in more than 60% of rising INDPRO observations.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with a low federal funds rate creates negative real yields and forces expectations of aggressive future tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the mechanism fails when CPIAUCSL growth decouples from core inflation measures.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows, or positive returns occur in less than 40% of months with PAYEMS growth.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive quarters of negative real GDP growth confirm a technical recession, triggering corporate earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 declines for two consecutive quarters
failure_condition: The market prices in recession risks prematurely, causing positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of confirmed recession quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in industrial production and retail sales indicate synchronized supply-side capacity expansion and demand absorption, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative returns in more than 50% of cases, or the predictive power vanishes when supply chain bottlenecks dominate and INDPRO rises while RSAFS falls.
